text.title: GraphQL Anti-Patterns
text.summary: Common GraphQL mistakes: N+1 without DataLoader, missing pagination, forgotten auth on new fields.

antipattern.description: Resolver that eagerly loads all relationships on the parent type, regardless of whether the client requested them.
antipattern.reason: Always-loaded relations execute queries even when the field is not selected, wasting DB resources on every request.

antipattern.description: List field that returns all items without pagination: `async def users(self) -> List[User]`.
antipattern.reason: Could return millions of rows in a single request, causing full table scans and OOM errors. All list fields that could grow must use cursor-based pagination.

antipattern.description: Adding a new sensitive field (email, apiKey, passwordHash) to a type without field-level authorization checks.
antipattern.reason: Any client that can query the parent type can retrieve the sensitive field. Auth audits should be automated via a schema lint rule.

antipattern.description: Leaving GraphQL introspection enabled in production without authentication.
antipattern.reason: Introspection exposes the full schema to attackers, enabling targeted injection and DoS attacks. Gate it behind admin auth or disable it entirely.
